From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

French painter

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/f/fc/Isidore_Pils_-_Portrait_de_l%27artiste.jpg/250px-Isidore_Pils_-_Portrait_de_l%27artiste.jpg)](/wiki/File:Isidore_Pils_-_Portrait_de_l%27artiste.jpg)

*Portrait of the artist*, self portrait, 1853

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/1/19/Self-Portrait_Isidore_Pils%29.jpg/250px-Self-Portrait_Isidore_Pils%29.jpg)](/wiki/File:Self-Portrait_Isidore_Pils).jpg)

Isidore Pils late self-portrait

**Isidore-Alexandre-Augustin Pils** (1815–1875) was a French academic painter of religious and military subjects.

## Biography

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Isidore_Pils&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Biography")]

Pils was born in Paris as the son of a soldier [François Pils](/wiki/Fran%C3%A7ois_Pils "François Pils"). At the age of twelve, he studied with [Guillaume Guillon-Lethière](/wiki/Guillaume_Guillon-Lethi%C3%A8re "Guillaume Guillon-Lethière") for four years.

In 1831 he became a student at the [École des Beaux-Arts](/wiki/%C3%89cole_des_Beaux-Arts "École des Beaux-Arts")[[1]](#cite_note-Grunchec_158-1) and studied under [François-Édouard Picot](/wiki/Fran%C3%A7ois-%C3%89douard_Picot "François-Édouard Picot").[[2]](#cite_note-Boime_27-2) He competed for the [Prix de Rome](/wiki/Prix_de_Rome "Prix de Rome"), which he won in 1838 for a history painting, *St. Peter Healing a Lame Man at the Door of the Temple*.[[3]](#cite_note-3) Although in poor health, Pils then spent the customary three years at the [French Academy in Rome](/wiki/French_Academy_in_Rome "French Academy in Rome") at the [Villa Medici](/wiki/Villa_Medici "Villa Medici"), which then had [Jean Auguste Dominique Ingres](/wiki/Jean_Auguste_Dominique_Ingres "Jean Auguste Dominique Ingres") as its director. While in Italy he visited Naples, Venice, and Florence.

Pils's earlier paintings have religious themes.[[1]](#cite_note-Grunchec_158-1) In 1849 he completed his most famous work, *[Rouget de L'Isle](/wiki/Claude_Joseph_Rouget_de_Lisle "Claude Joseph Rouget de Lisle") Singing [La Marseillaise](/wiki/La_Marseillaise "La Marseillaise")*, which now resides at the [Musée historique de Strasbourg](/wiki/Mus%C3%A9e_historique_de_Strasbourg "Musée historique de Strasbourg"). After experiences travelling with French troops through the Crimea, his themes took on military and nationalistic subjects. He later produced many military scenes during the siege of Paris during the [Franco-Prussian War](/wiki/Franco-Prussian_War "Franco-Prussian War") of 1870.

Pils was appointed professor of painting at the École des Beaux-Arts in 1863 but left the same year for two years in Algeria. In 1868 he was elected to seat #14 of the [Académie des Beaux-Arts](/wiki/Acad%C3%A9mie_des_Beaux-Arts "Académie des Beaux-Arts"). Among his students were [Adrien Moreau](/wiki/Adrien_Moreau "Adrien Moreau"), [Paul Adolphe Rajon](/wiki/Paul_Adolphe_Rajon "Paul Adolphe Rajon"), [Julien Dupré](/wiki/Julien_Dupr%C3%A9 "Julien Dupré"), [Luc-Olivier Merson](/wiki/Luc-Olivier_Merson "Luc-Olivier Merson"), [Ludovic Piette](/wiki/Ludovic_Piette "Ludovic Piette"), [Édouard Joseph Dantan](/wiki/%C3%89douard_Joseph_Dantan "Édouard Joseph Dantan") and [László Mednyánszky](/wiki/L%C3%A1szl%C3%B3_Medny%C3%A1nszky "László Mednyánszky") Henry-Lionel Brioux.[[4]](#cite_note-4)

Among his other work, Pils painted part of the ceiling of the grand staircase of the [Palais Garnier](/wiki/Palais_Garnier "Palais Garnier"), in four panels titled *The Gods of Olympus*, *Apollo in His Chariot*, *Triumph of Harmony*, and *Apotheosis of the Opera*, completed the year of his death. The following year he was the subject of a biography by [Louis Becq de Fouquières](/wiki/Louis_Becq_de_Fouqui%C3%A8res "Louis Becq de Fouquières").

Pils died in [Douarnenez](/wiki/Douarnenez "Douarnenez")[[2]](#cite_note-Boime_27-2) and is buried in section 54 of [Père Lachaise Cemetery](/wiki/P%C3%A8re_Lachaise_Cemetery "Père Lachaise Cemetery").
